title: Beast Mode Eva 02
wikitext:
[]: Beast Mode Eva-02 Post by UltraBlackPilot » Wed Jul 15, 2009 2:57 pm I saw eight minutes of 2.0 on Youtube, And what was up with beast mode? It contorted Unit 2? What was up with those rods, and did Mari fire that cannon with nails from the 2's pylon? and who even thought of that kind of a feature?

[]: Re: Beast Mode {possibly might see some spoilers} Post by bladerj » Wed Jul 15, 2009 3:14 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: I saw eight minutes of 2.0 on Youtube, And what was up with beast mode? It contorted Unit 2? What was up with those rods, and did Mari fire that cannon with nails from the 2's pylon? and who even thought of that kind of a feature?

[]: Post by Ornette » Wed Jul 15, 2009 3:15 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=258113#258113

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 15, 2009 4:32 pm its what happens when Anno falls asleep watching the newly released Beast Wars DVD. I guess Eva can have some sort of Beast mode since I mean they are restrained beast. Maybe 02 wanted this to happen, wanted the ability to go apeshit.

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 15, 2009 4:39 pm Kyoko used to read Spider-Man . I think that's the only real explanation. We can explain the physical deformation aspects -- "Eva that abandons its humanity", blah blah, probably means there's some shapeshifting involved -- but those "limiters" will never, ever make any sense. Look at the size of those fucking things. Where the heck do they fit? (Aren't there supposed to be bones, muscles, and organs there?) Sigh. EDIT: I fixed my fixed title so that it more properly reflects the subject matter. ***** **** is obviously "Beast Mode" (though is that even an official term...?).

[]: Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 15, 2009 5:21 pm Reichu wrote: We can explain the physical deformation aspects -- "Eva that abandons its humanity", blah blah, probably means there's some shapeshifting involved -- but those "limiters" will never, ever make any sense. Look at the size of those fucking things. Where the heck do they fit? (Aren't there supposed to be bones, muscles, and organs there?) Sigh.

[]: Post by wonderluster » Wed Jul 15, 2009 5:30 pm so do you think Unit 02 was conscious of the battle or was Mari controlling the way the mouth moved as well? because in just about all the other berserker scenes, you never see the pilot because the Eva's the one in control. You think maybe this could have been like the Bardiel fight except Mari was the D.P. and Unit 02 was the one powerless to do anything?

[]: Post by CyberXIII » Wed Jul 15, 2009 5:50 pm I Accidentally Dracula wrote: Since Rebuild's primary goal was to "modernize" Evangelion for today's audience (that is, take a relatively deep and intelligent break-through series from the '90s and turn it into an absolutely generic mecha series where anybody can pilot anybody else's robot, the robots themselves are simply machines, and the laughable physics boil down to "it works because it does"), Anno has taken certain liberties. In other words, unexplained mass-shifting a la Transformers.

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 15, 2009 5:58 pm CyberXIII wrote: Lastly, mass shifting has been present since the original series. One minute the Evas tower over skyscrapers, the next they're hiding behind and climbing them.

[]: Post by Barong » Wed Jul 15, 2009 6:09 pm How this ability could be used to explain Eva-02's ability to accommodate gigantic control rods and still function anatomically, I have absolutely no idea.

[]: Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 15, 2009 6:28 pm Since when does NGE's physics make sense? If the laws of physic did apply, then the Evas wouldn't exist in the first place.

[]: Post by Axell » Wed Jul 15, 2009 6:30 pm hmm, do evas have bones? other than teeth i cant remember any instance of bones showing. Arms have been broken, and they obviously have a rigid structure, but who knows what cool properties pwm has

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 15, 2009 6:34 pm You should re~watch EoE when 02 gets died. I see bones and such all over the place.

[]: Post by Axell » Wed Jul 15, 2009 6:40 pm woops :P my memory fails me yet again

[]: Post by evilhead008 » Wed Jul 15, 2009 7:01 pm Er.. I forgot how to resize it. Sorry..

[]: Post by Axell » Wed Jul 15, 2009 7:03 pm i already said i was wrong....

[]: Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 15, 2009 7:19 pm Axell wrote: hmm, do evas have bones? other than teeth i cant remember any instance of bones showing. Arms have been broken, and they obviously have a rigid structure, but who knows what cool properties pwm has

[]: Post by Rock-Slash » Wed Jul 15, 2009 7:26 pm Axell wrote: hmm, do evas have bones? other than teeth i cant remember any instance of bones showing. Arms have been broken, and they obviously have a rigid structure, but who knows what cool properties pwm has

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 15, 2009 7:27 pm The Eva's have bones though we only seem to see the spine and the skull and some arms here and there.

[]: Post by Soul Fire » Wed Jul 15, 2009 7:31 pm Eva's are organic but have always been capable of magical mind bending things. They can regenerate body parts in battle, withstand blasts from N2 mines and so questioning the organic structutes at all is moot at they are cabable of strange things either way. Those control rods looked just about able to fit in there and we dont really know that eva's have organs other than brain and muscle tissues. Perhaps this is where the cores come in, contoling bodilly functions? I can just about .....just about, see those control rods being there. On the subject of these little discrepancies turing Eva into a generic mecha show, I think this isnt fair at all. The one thing eva will never be is a generic mecha show. To be honest from what I have seen, they have done a very good job on this new movie. It still ultimatley feels like Eva whilst your watching it and it still has those trade mark trolls we know and love Anno for. Ive not even watched the whole thing through but I can still say it has a lot of the things I liked about eva still present. This latest rebuild movie has some moments in it together with the music and sound effects, that shock and make me sit there thinking ' oh shit what was that'. I listened to the sound recording of the whole film the other day and I skipped to the Asuka having a bad day scene and just listening to that scene without having seen the animation was enough to make me realise this was still ultimatley evangelion. On the subject of the bones, there was an entire Eva graveyard filled with them in the origional series if I remember rightly. If you look carefully in this new film in the long camrip, when unit 1 gets it's arm severed and Shinji pushes the angel into the elevator, you can see the bone in the middle of the severed arm. Dean

 questioning the organic structutes at all is moot at they are cabable of strange things either way. Those control rods looked just about able to fit in there and we dont really know that eva's have organs other than brain and muscle tissues. Perhaps this is where the cores come in, contoling bodilly functions? I can just about .....just about, see those control rods being there. On the subject of these little discrepancies turing Eva into a generic mecha show, I think this isnt fair at all. The one thing eva will never be is a generic mecha show. To be honest from what I have seen, they have done a very good job on this new movie. It still ultimatley feels like Eva whilst your watching it and it still has those trade mark trolls we know and love Anno for. Ive not even watched the whole thing through but I can still say it has a lot of the things I liked about eva still present. This latest rebuild movie has some moments in it together with the music and sound effects, that shock and make me sit there thinking ' oh shit what was that'. I listened to the sound recording of the whole film the other day and I skipped to the Asuka having a bad day scene and just listening to that scene without having seen the animation was enough to make me realise this was still ultimatley evangelion. On the subject of the bones, there was an entire Eva graveyard filled with them in the origional series if I remember rightly. If you look carefully in this new film in the long camrip, when unit 1 gets it's arm severed and Shinji pushes the angel into the elevator, you can see the bone in the middle of the severed arm.
